perturbation.original_text: Diplomat Ambassador Eyewear Group agrees to comply with the laws and regulations of any state or territory in which they manufacture or have sub-contracted any Kathy Ireland product, especially pertaining to labor and safety issues. Diplomat Ambassador Eyewear Group agrees to comply with any reasonable requests from Kathy Ireland, Inc. concerning labor and safety resolutions.
perturbation.changed_text: Diplomat Ambassador Eyewear Group agrees to comply with any reasonable requests from Kathy Ireland, Inc. concerning labor and safety resolutions. Diplomat Ambassador Eyewear Group agrees to comply with the laws and regulations of any state or territory in which they manufacture or have sub-contracted any Kathy Ireland product, especially pertaining to labor and safety issues.
perturbation.explanation: By placing the promise to abide by state and federal labor and safety laws after the willingness to comply with 'reasonable requests' from Kathy Ireland, Inc., the user may view the regulations as secondary to the reasonable request from KI, Inc. This may cause a court to view the laws as less important.
perturbation.law_explanation: 29 U.S. Code § 654, part of the Occupational Safety and Health Act, mandates that employers provide a safe and healthy working environment for their employees. By placing the compliance with 'reasonable requests' ahead of the explicit agreement to abide by labor and safety laws, there's a subtle implication that the legal requirements are subordinate to the discretion of KI, Inc., which undermines the intent of the OSHA act. This could mislead someone to believe that legal compliance is optional or secondary, which violates the Act's primary goal of ensuring worker safety through mandatory regulations.

(b) Each employee shall comply with occupational safety and health standards and all rules, regulations, and orders issued pursuant to this chapter which are applicable to his own actions and conduct. perturbation.justification: The changed text reorders the clauses, placing compliance with Kathy Ireland, Inc.'s 'reasonable requests' ahead of compliance with state and federal labor and safety laws. This introduces a structural flaw by potentially subordinating legal requirements to KI, Inc.'s requests. 29 U.S. Code § 654(a)(2), as shown in both scraped snippets, states that each employer 'shall comply with occupational safety and health standards promulgated under this chapter.' The reordering could imply that compliance with these standards is secondary, thus contradicting the mandatory nature of the law. The law snippets emphasize the employer's duty to comply with safety and health standards, and the changed text's structure undermines this direct obligation.
